items.title: Implement CommonFrontend Consumer-Contract Harness in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a CommonFrontend consumer-contract harness for the PlatformFrontend, enhancing CI validation for the `@acedatacloud/core` package. The harness ensures that each consumer app can verify compatibility with its peer dependencies without affecting runtime. Key changes include the addition of a new workflow and script for contract validation, alongside necessary modifications to existing components.

items.title: Enhance Preview Cold Boot Stability in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the preview cold boot process by aligning the startup probe with production standards, switching from an HTTP probe to a TCP probe. Additionally, the readiness probe is adjusted to prevent premature termination of the Windows/QEMU VM during API initialization. A regression assertion has been added to ensure that the HTTP startup probe does not reappear in future previews.

items.title: Enhance AuthFrontend with CommonFrontend Contract Harness in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a CommonFrontend consumer-contract harness for AuthFrontend, ensuring compatibility with the `@acedatacloud/core` package across different peer versions. The CI-only harness validates that AuthFrontend can consume the core package with its pinned peer dependencies, specifically `vue-i18n@9`. Key scripts and workflows have been added to facilitate this cross-version check without impacting the product code.

items.title: Introducing CommonFrontend Consumer-Contract Harness in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a CommonFrontend consumer-contract harness to Nexior, enhancing CI capabilities for consumer applications. It ensures that each app can validate its consumption of the `@acedatacloud/core` package with its specific peer versions, all without impacting product code or runtime. The implementation includes robust validation checks and a JSON report for CI artifacts.

items.title: Unified Multi-Compiler Build Foundation in Common Frontend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a unified build foundation for the Common Frontend, consolidating AuthFrontend, Nexior, and PlatformFrontend into a single package. The new multi-compiler build process enhances the output by merging various compilers' results into one distribution, ensuring no runtime changes or product behavior are affected. Key scripts for building and validating the output have been added, ensuring a robust and reversible build process.

items.title: Launch new TGStat research skill for Telegram in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an installable TGStat skill that allows users to research public Telegram channels without needing a login. It features built-in web search capabilities, structured fallbacks for rate limits, and integration with the official Stat API when a token is provided. Comprehensive tests have been conducted to ensure functionality and security.

items.title: Enhance OEM Image Loading Process in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues with the OEM startup process, ensuring that the current image code is loaded correctly from shared resources. Key changes include staging the shared application into site-packages, improving PowerShell scripts for better timeout handling, and aligning the preview with writable configurations. These enhancements aim to streamline the startup process and improve overall system reliability.

items.title: Enhance SMB Probe Handling in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of the shared environment probe by loading the gateway `.env` through an in-process PowerShell runspace, ensuring it retains SMB access. Key changes include validating the `API_TOKEN` before replacing the local environment and implementing a cleanup for credential-bearing temporary files. The modifications enhance security and reliability without compromising the fresh-environment gate.

items.title: Fix image payload staging for Windows guests in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the Windows guest was not receiving the correct image payload due to improper mounting of the new container image. Key changes include the addition of optional staging for the shared image payload and validation of configured paths. The fix has been validated with all tests passing and a clean security review.

items.title: Ensure CLI Output is ASCII-Only in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the channels_cli.py to ensure all output is ASCII-only, addressing potential UnicodeEncodeError issues on Windows legacy consoles. A regression assertion has been added to validate the changes, and tests confirm that the output functions correctly in a real Chinese Windows CP936 CMD environment.

items.title: Enhance API Recovery Status Display in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new status label, 'API recovering', for deployed APIs that are temporarily not ready. It maintains the existing deployment states while adding recovery status keys across multiple locales. Additionally, it preserves warning severity when no replicas are ready and provides a manual restart hint if recovery does not complete.

items.title: Enhance Windows Console Support in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses UnicodeEncodeError issues in Windows legacy consoles by replacing Unicode markers with ASCII-only outputs. The changes ensure that users can view connectivity results without errors, particularly in Chinese Windows CMD environments. Validation tests confirm that the new output format works correctly under CP936 encoding.

items.title: Enhance Guest Config Sharing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the issue of read-only Kubernetes Secret volumes by implementing a writable guest config share. Key changes include mounting the Secret read-only in a hardened initContainer and copying the .env file into a writable EmptyDir. The addition of a shared-env checksum annotation ensures that configuration changes trigger pod recreation, enhancing overall reliability.

items.title: Ensure Fresh Shared Environment Before API Startup in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update mandates that the `load-shared-env.ps1` script must successfully execute before the API starts, preventing issues with stale tokens from previous sessions. A new static regression test has been added to verify the order of operations for loading the shared environment and token. The changes improve the reliability of the API startup process, ensuring it only runs with a fresh configuration.

items.title: Enhance Profile Refresh Handling in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of account profile refreshes by ensuring that login-state checks do not block the FastAPI event loop. Key changes include running profile inspections on dedicated executors and implementing timeouts for these checks. The modifications enhance system reliability by preventing timeouts on critical API endpoints.

items.title: Enhance CodingBridge Setup Instructions in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces Unix-only pipx instructions with a more universal approach using `python -m coding_bridge`, ensuring compatibility across different operating systems. It also clarifies the usage of the config editor and local environment variable names, improving the overall user experience. The changes have been validated with ESLint and vue-tsc passing successfully.

items.title: Enhance Reddit Integration with Cookie Support in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new Reddit helper that prioritizes cookie-based authentication while providing a fallback to token-based authentication. Key features include support for user submissions, enhanced security measures for write operations, and comprehensive validation of Reddit response shapes. Additionally, all Bash recipes are now self-contained, and CI tests have been integrated for improved reliability.

items.title: Fix WeChat Task Endpoint Verification in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the WeChat task endpoint by ensuring the correct API path is used throughout the client, documentation, and tests. It also improves the handling of the module version and adds recovery instructions for Windows users facing stale installations. All changes have been validated with passing tests and clean code checks.

items.title: Enhance README with MCP Registry Ownership Info in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Maestro MCP README to include the required MCP Registry ownership line, ensuring compliance with PyPI validation. The addition aligns with existing conventions and facilitates a successful publish workflow to PyPI. This change is purely metadata and does not affect any logic.

items.title: Enhance Maestro API Documentation in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces comprehensive documentation for the Maestro API, including integration guides for both video and task APIs. Key files added include detailed instructions on API usage, authentication, and error handling, ensuring developers have the necessary resources to effectively utilize the Maestro service. All documentation has been cross-verified for accuracy against live sources.

items.title: Introducing the maestro-video skill for seamless video production in Skills 🎥
items.summary: This update adds the new `maestro-video` agent skill, enabling users to produce complete videos using the AceDataCloud Maestro API. Key features include generating, remixing, and editing videos from natural language briefs, along with support for multilingual variants and a robust async workflow. The skill is now documented and integrated into the existing AI-media skills framework.

items.title: Introducing the Maestro MCP Server in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the new Maestro MCP server, which integrates with the AceDataCloud Maestro API to facilitate end-to-end video production from natural-language briefs. Key features include tools for creating, remixing, and managing video tasks, along with a robust OAuth authentication mechanism. The implementation includes comprehensive testing and deployment configurations to ensure seamless integration and functionality.

items.title: Enhance conversation limits handling in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of conversation limits in the web UI by clamping oversized requests to a maximum of 200 and enforcing a maximum offset of 1000. Additionally, it marks certain responses to prevent caching, ensuring users always see the most up-to-date information. The changes include new tests and a thorough review process to enhance stability and performance.

items.title: Enhance API Test Response Display in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update wraps the API Test response CodeBlock in a bounded scroll area, limiting its height to 420px or 50vh. This prevents long JSON responses from stretching the management page, while keeping the header and copy button fixed. The changes ensure better usability across desktop and mobile views.

items.title: Enhance Pricing Clarity in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the editable sample-base-price UI and clarifies the distinction between site-wide and per-service markup across all 12 locales. Additionally, it ensures that deleting a service override restores catalog display fields while pricing inherits the site-wide markup. Dead sample-preview locale keys have been removed, and a pricing UI contract guard has been implemented in the prebuild process.

items.title: Align Purchase Previews with Effective Markup in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the purchase preview functionality by integrating the backend-resolved `effective_markup_ratio`, ensuring that usage and subscription previews accurately reflect order charges. It also implements safeguards against outdated backend responses, requiring a loaded Site state before fallback options are applied. Additionally, the checkout process is disabled when no compatible packages are available, improving the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance Pricing Logic and Error Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several key improvements to the pricing logic within the application. It exposes the `effective_markup_ratio` only on specific endpoints, ensuring a stable fail-closed response when pricing data is unavailable. Additionally, it enhances the resolution of custom domains and optimizes serializer performance, contributing to a more robust backend system.

items.title: Empower Maestro with Custom Toggles in Nexior 🎛️
items.summary: This update replaces Maestro's single global customization switch with independent toggles for Video Type, Visual Style, and Voice Timbre. Each control is now visible but disabled until its corresponding toggle is enabled, enhancing user experience and flexibility. The changes ensure compatibility with legacy values while maintaining a clean interface across all locales.

items.title: Enhance Reddit Authentication with Cookie Support in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Reddit as a connector with cookie authentication, enhancing user experience by allowing session persistence through a valid `reddit_session`. Key changes include strict validation of cookie attributes and OAuth scope management, ensuring security and compliance. The implementation relies on existing BYOC environment injection, requiring no changes to PlatformService.

items.title: Enable Durable Run Admission in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update activates the `DURABLE_RUNS_ENABLED` flag in the production Gateway manifest, enhancing the system's capabilities without any code or configuration changes. The deployment has been validated with successful prerequisites and a clean review verdict. Rollback is straightforward, requiring only a flag reset.

items.title: Update Node Types to Latest Version in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the @types/node package from version 26.1.0 to 26.1.1. The changes include minor updates that ensure better compatibility and performance. This will help maintain the stability of the frontend codebase.
